title.before: Test: All FFI functions declared with [LibraryImport("wgsocket")]
title.after: Test: All LibraryImport declarations resolve correctly

body.before:
Verify acceptance criterion for story US-WNE-9:

> All FFI functions declared with [LibraryImport("wgsocket")]
body.after: Write unit tests that verify every P/Invoke method in NativeMethods.cs has the [LibraryImport("wgsocket")] attribute via reflection. Assert that no method uses [DllImport]. Verify the partial class structure is present. Use a Roslyn analyzer test or reflection-based test to enumerate all extern methods.

title.before: Test: Correct marshalling attributes for string and pointer and buffer parameters
title.after: Test: Marshalling attributes are correct for all P/Invoke parameters

body.before:
Verify acceptance criterion for story US-WNE-9:

> Correct marshalling attributes for string and pointer and buffer parameters
body.after: Write reflection-based tests that verify marshalling attributes on each P/Invoke parameter: string parameters have UTF-8 marshalling, pointer parameters use nint or unsafe byte*, buffer parameters pair a pointer with a length parameter. Verify return types match expected C ABI types.

title.before: Test: Compiles with .NET 8 source generator
title.after: Test: AOT source generator produces no warnings

body.before:
Verify acceptance criterion for story US-WNE-9:

> Compiles with .NET 8 source generator
body.after: Create a test that builds the interop project with PublishAot=true and verifies zero SYSLIB1054/SYSLIB1051 warnings. Can be an integration test or MSBuild target that fails the build on source generator diagnostics. Verify the generated marshalling code exists in the obj directory.

title.before: Test: No legacy DllImport usage anywhere in codebase
title.after: Test: No legacy DllImport usage in codebase

body.before:
Verify acceptance criterion for story US-WNE-9:

> No legacy DllImport usage anywhere in codebase
body.after: Write a test that scans all .cs source files in the project for [DllImport] attributes and fails if any are found. Use Roslyn source analysis or simple text search. This acts as a regression gate to ensure no one accidentally adds legacy DllImport declarations.

title.before: Test: WgDeviceHandle extends SafeHandle for device lifetime management
title.after: Test: WgDeviceHandle lifecycle and IsInvalid

body.before:
Verify acceptance criterion for story US-WNE-10:

> WgDeviceHandle extends SafeHandle for device lifetime management
body.after: Test that WgDeviceHandle reports IsInvalid=true for IntPtr.Zero. Test that a valid handle returns IsInvalid=false. Verify that calling Dispose sets IsClosed=true. Mock or stub wg_device_free to verify ReleaseHandle calls it exactly once. Test double-dispose does not throw.

title.before: Test: WgSocketHandle extends SafeHandle for socket fd lifetime
title.after: Test: WgSocketHandle lifecycle and platform-conditional IsInvalid

body.before:
Verify acceptance criterion for story US-WNE-10:

> WgSocketHandle extends SafeHandle for socket fd lifetime
body.after: Test that WgSocketHandle reports IsInvalid=true for -1 on Unix and INVALID_HANDLE_VALUE on Windows. Verify ReleaseHandle calls wg_socket_close. Test that valid fd values return IsInvalid=false. Test double-dispose is safe.

title.before: Test: ReleaseHandle calls wg_device_free and wg_close respectively
title.after: Test: ReleaseHandle calls correct native free function

body.before:
Verify acceptance criterion for story US-WNE-10:

> ReleaseHandle calls wg_device_free and wg_close respectively
body.after: Using a test double or mock native library, verify that WgDeviceHandle.ReleaseHandle invokes wg_device_free with the correct handle value, and WgSocketHandle.ReleaseHandle invokes wg_socket_close with the correct fd. Verify the return value of ReleaseHandle reflects the native call success/failure.

title.before: Test: Prevent use-after-dispose via ObjectDisposedException
title.after: Test: ObjectDisposedException on use-after-dispose

body.before:
Verify acceptance criterion for story US-WNE-10:

> Prevent use-after-dispose via ObjectDisposedException
body.after: Dispose a WgDeviceHandle and WgSocketHandle, then attempt to use them in a P/Invoke call (or via the ThrowIfDisposed helper). Verify ObjectDisposedException is thrown with a message identifying the handle type. Test both explicit Dispose and finalization paths where possible.

title.before: Test: CriticalFinalizerObject ensures cleanup even on abrupt termination
title.after: Test: CriticalFinalizerObject cleanup guarantee

body.before:
Verify acceptance criterion for story US-WNE-10:

> CriticalFinalizerObject ensures cleanup even on abrupt termination
body.after: Verify that SafeHandle subclasses inherit CriticalFinalizerObject behavior. Test that the handle type is assignable to CriticalFinalizerObject via reflection. Create a handle, drop all managed references without calling Dispose, force GC.Collect and GC.WaitForPendingFinalizers, then verify the native free function was called (via a test counter or mock).

title.before: Test: WgException base class with ErrorCode property
title.after: Test: WgException hierarchy structure and ErrorCode property

body.before:
Verify acceptance criterion for story US-WNE-11:

> WgException base class with ErrorCode property
body.after: Verify WgException extends Exception and has an int ErrorCode property. Test each subclass (WgInvalidHandleException, WgConnectionRefusedException, WgBufferTooSmallException, WgKeyException, WgTimeoutException) extends WgException. Verify default messages are set. Verify serialization round-trips preserve ErrorCode. Test constructor overloads (message, message+inner).

title.before: Test: Specific subclasses: WgInvalidHandleException and WgConnectionRefusedException and WgBufferTooSmallException
title.after: Test: Correct exception subclass thrown for each error code

body.before:
Verify acceptance criterion for story US-WNE-11:

> Specific subclasses: WgInvalidHandleException and WgConnectionRefusedException and WgBufferTooSmallException
body.after: Test that ThrowIfError maps each known error code to the correct exception subclass. Use parameterized tests with (errorCode, expectedExceptionType) pairs. Verify unknown negative error codes throw base WgException with the code preserved. Verify positive and zero codes do not throw.

title.before: Test: Native error detail string from wg_last_error included in exception message
title.after: Test: Native error string from wg_last_error included in exception message

body.before:
Verify acceptance criterion for story US-WNE-11:

> Native error detail string from wg_last_error included in exception message
body.after: Mock wg_last_error to return a known error string. Call ThrowIfError with a negative code. Verify the thrown exception's Message property contains the native error string. Test with null/empty native error string to verify graceful fallback. Test the context overload includes the call-site context in the message.

title.before: Test: Helper method that checks return code and throws if negative
title.after: Test: ThrowIfError returns cleanly on success codes

body.before:
Verify acceptance criterion for story US-WNE-11:

> Helper method that checks return code and throws if negative
body.after: Verify ThrowIfError does not throw for return code 0 (success). Verify it does not throw for positive return codes (e.g., bytes-read counts). Verify it throws for -1, -2, and other negative values. Benchmark to confirm no allocation on the success path.

title.before: Test: Send operations accept ReadOnlySpan<byte>
title.after: Test: Send operations accept ReadOnlySpan and pin correctly

body.before:
Verify acceptance criterion for story US-WNE-12:

> Send operations accept ReadOnlySpan<byte>
body.after: Test that BufferMarshaller.PinAndSend accepts a ReadOnlySpan<byte> and passes the pinned pointer to the native send function. Verify ArgumentException on empty span. Verify the returned byte count matches the native return value. Use a mock native layer to capture the pointer and length arguments and verify they match the input span.

title.before: Test: Receive operations write to Span<byte>
title.after: Test: Receive operations write to Span and report actual length

body.before:
Verify acceptance criterion for story US-WNE-12:

> Receive operations write to Span<byte>
body.after: Test that BufferMarshaller.Receive writes into a Span<byte> and returns actual bytes received. Verify ArgumentException on empty span. Test WgBufferTooSmallException is thrown when native returns buffer-too-small error code. Verify the span contents match what the mock native layer wrote.

title.before: Test: Pin managed buffers during P/Invoke calls using GCHandle or fixed
title.after: Test: Buffer pinning prevents GC relocation during P/Invoke

body.before:
Verify acceptance criterion for story US-WNE-12:

> Pin managed buffers during P/Invoke calls using GCHandle or fixed
body.after: Verify that during a simulated P/Invoke call the buffer address remains stable. Allocate a buffer, pin it via the marshalling helper, trigger GC.Collect inside a callback, and verify the pointer has not changed. This tests both the fixed statement and GCHandle pinning paths.

title.before: Test: Config strings marshalled as UTF-8 null-terminated
title.after: Test: UTF-8 config string marshalling with null termination

body.before:
Verify acceptance criterion for story US-WNE-12:

> Config strings marshalled as UTF-8 null-terminated
body.after: Test ConfigMarshaller.ToNativeUtf8 produces a null-terminated UTF-8 byte sequence. Verify ASCII strings, multi-byte UTF-8 characters (e.g., emoji, CJK), and empty strings. Verify that strings exceeding 256 bytes use ArrayPool rental and are returned after use. Verify the null terminator is present at the correct position.

title.before: Test: Key material accepted as ReadOnlySpan<byte> (32 bytes) and never allocated as managed string
title.after: Test: Key material validated as 32-byte ReadOnlySpan and never string-allocated

body.before:
Verify acceptance criterion for story US-WNE-12:

> Key material accepted as ReadOnlySpan<byte> (32 bytes) and never allocated as managed string
body.after: Test KeyMarshaller.ValidateAndPin accepts exactly 32-byte ReadOnlySpan<byte>. Verify ArgumentException for spans of length 0, 31, 33, and 64. Verify the pinned pointer matches the span data. Confirm via reflection or code review that no ToString or Encoding.GetString calls exist in the key marshalling path. Test that the key bytes are not observable in managed string intern pool.

title.before: Test: Library resolves correctly from runtimes/{rid}/native/ in NuGet package
title.after: Test: Library resolves from runtimes/{rid}/native/ path

body.before:
Verify acceptance criterion for story US-WNE-13:

> Library resolves correctly from runtimes/{rid}/native/ in NuGet package
body.after: Test that the custom DllImportResolver probes the correct runtimes/{rid}/native/ path based on RuntimeInformation.RuntimeIdentifier. Mock the file system or NativeLibrary.TryLoad to verify the expected paths are probed in order. Verify the resolver returns a valid handle when the library exists at the expected path.

title.before: Test: DllNotFoundException provides helpful message about missing native binary for current platform
title.after: Test: DllNotFoundException contains helpful diagnostic message

body.before:
Verify acceptance criterion for story US-WNE-13:

> DllNotFoundException provides helpful message about missing native binary for current platform
body.after: Test that when the native library is not found, the thrown DllNotFoundException message includes: the current RuntimeIdentifier, the expected file path with platform-correct extension (.so/.dylib/.dll), and a suggestion to install WgSocket.Native.{rid}. Test on the current platform and verify the extension matches.

title.before: Test: Works on win-x64 and linux-x64 and osx-x64 and osx-arm64
title.after: Test: Platform RID resolution covers all supported targets

body.before:
Verify acceptance criterion for story US-WNE-13:

> Works on win-x64 and linux-x64 and osx-x64 and osx-arm64
body.after: Parameterized test that verifies the resolver produces correct probe paths for win-x64, linux-x64, osx-x64, and osx-arm64. Verify library file extension is correct per platform (.dll, .so, .dylib). Verify fallback RID logic (e.g., linux-musl-x64 falls back to linux-x64).

title.before: Test: Custom NativeLibrary.SetDllImportResolver if needed for non-standard paths
title.after: Test: Custom resolver falls back to default when custom probing fails

body.before:
Verify acceptance criterion for story US-WNE-13:

> Custom NativeLibrary.SetDllImportResolver if needed for non-standard paths
body.after: Verify that when the custom DllImportResolver does not find the library in runtimes/{rid}/native/, it returns IntPtr.Zero to allow the default .NET resolution to proceed. Test that the default resolver can still find the library if it is on the system PATH or in the app directory.
